title: fix: make footer logo redirect to homepage
body:
## Description

Fixed the footer logo navigation issue.

### Changes Made

* Replaced the footer logo wrapper from a static `span` to a `Link` component from `react-router-dom`.
* Added navigation to `/` when the footer logo is clicked.
* Preserved all existing styling and classes.

### Testing

* Verified that clicking the footer logo from other pages (e.g. `/upload`) redirects users to the homepage.
* Confirmed no styling regressions.

Fixes #640

Release notes

Sourced from @​tailwindcss/vite's releases.

Added

  • Add --silent option to suppress output in @tailwindcss/cli (#20100)

Fixed

  • Remove deprecation warnings by using Module#registerHooks instead of Module#register on Node 26+ (#20028)
  • Canonicalization: don't crash when plugin utilities throw for unsupported values (#20052)
  • Allow @apply to be used with CSS mixins (#19427)
  • Ensure not-* correctly negates @container queries, including style(…) queries (#20059)
  • Ensure drop-shadow-* color utilities work with custom shadow values containing calc(…) (#20080)
  • Fix 'Sourcemap is likely to be incorrect' warnings when using @tailwindcss/vite (#20103)
  • Ensure @tailwindcss/webpack can be installed in Rspack projects without requiring webpack as a peer dependency (#20027)
  • Canonicalization: don't suggest invalid calc(…) expressions (e.g. px-[calc(1rem+0px)] → px-[calc(1rem+0)]) (#20127)
  • Canonicalization: avoid suggesting large spacing-scale values for arbitrary lengths (e.g. left-[99999px] → left-[99999px], not left-24999.75) (#20130)
  • Ensure @tailwindcss/cli in --watch mode recovers when a tracked dependency is deleted and restored (#20137)
  • Ensure standalone @tailwindcss/cli binaries are ignored when scanning for class candidates (#20139)
  • Ensure class candidates are extracted from Twig addClass(…) and removeClass(…) calls (#20198)
  • Don't crash in the Ruby or Vue preprocessors when scanning files containing invalid UTF-8 bytes (#19588)
  • Allow @variant to be used inside addBase (#19480)
  • Ensure @source globs with symlinks are preserved (#20203)
  • Ensure later @source rules can re-include files excluded by earlier @source not rules (#20203)
  • Upgrade: don't migrate empty class rules to invalid @utility rules (#20205)
  • Ensure transitions between inset-shadow-none and other inset shadows work correctly (#20208)
  • Ensure explicitly referenced @source directories are scanned even when ignored by git (#20214)

title: Feat: Add multi-recipient sharing with individual revocation control
body:
## Summary

Implements a comprehensive multi-recipient password sharing system with individual access control and revocation capabilities. Users can now share passwords with multiple recipients simultaneously while maintaining independent control over each recipient's access, including the ability to revoke access for specific individuals without affecting others.

## Changes

### Database Models

**PasswordShare Model** (`src/models/PasswordShare.js`)
- Represents a password share for multiple recipients
- Tracks share metadata (owner, expiration, access limits)
- Supports per-share access count limits
- Maintains IP address log and total access tracking

**ShareRecipient Model** (`src/models/ShareRecipient.js`)
- Tracks individual recipient access and control
- Per-recipient unique access tokens for security
- Independent status tracking (pending, accepted, accessed, revoked)
- Detailed access history with IP and user agent logging
- Individual revocation tracking with reason and timestamp

### Service Layer

**MultiRecipientService** (`src/services/multiRecipientService.js`) provides:
- `createMultiRecipientShare()` - Create share for up to 100 recipients
- `addRecipients()` - Add recipients to existing active share
- `revokeRecipient()` - Revoke individual recipient access
- `revokeAllRecipients()` - Revoke all recipients at once
- `recordAccess()` - Log access with IP/user agent tracking
- `checkAccessStatus()` - Verify access validity
- `getShareDetails()` - Retrieve complete share information with stats
- `getAccessAnalytics()` - Generate analytics and access timeline
- `cleanupExpiredShares()` - Automated cleanup of expired shares

### Key Features

**Individual Revocation**
- Revoke specific recipient without affecting others
- Revocation reason and timestamp tracked
- Independent status per recipient
- Audit trail for compliance

**Access Control**
- Unique access token per recipient
- Per-recipient access count limits
- Per-share global access limits
- Automatic expiration enforcement
- Access status validation

**Access Tracking**
- IP address and user agent logging
- First and last access timestamps
- Detailed access history per recipient
- Total access count per share
- IP address aggregation for analytics

**Recipient Management**
- Add recipients to existing active shares
- Normalize email addresses (lowercase)
- Support for up to 100 recipients per share
- Bulk revocation capability
- Email uniqueness per share

**Scalability**
- MongoDB indexing for performance
- Efficient query patterns
- Support for large recipient lists
- Batch operations
- Automatic cleanup of expired data

### Test Coverage

Comprehensive test suite with 20+ test cases covering:
- Multi-recipient share creation with validation
- Recipient addition with limits
- Individual recipient revocation
- Bulk revocation operations
- Access tracking and recording
- Access count limit enforcement
- Share expiration validation
- Email normalization
- Status transitions
- Error scenarios

### API Documentation

Complete API documentation (`src/docs/MULTI_RECIPIENT_API.md`) including:
- Database schema specifications
- RESTful endpoint documentation
- Request/response examples
- Error handling guidelines
- Integration examples
- Security best practices
- Feature overview
- Scalability considerations

## Security Considerations

- Each recipient receives a unique cryptographically random access token (32 bytes)
- Passwords encrypted in database
- No plaintext password storage
- IP address and user agent tracking for anomaly detection
- Automatic expiration enforced
- Revocation is immediate and irreversible
- Per-recipient access limits prevent abuse
- Integrates with password content validation from issue #697

## Integration Ready

The service layer is designed for easy integration with Express.js controllers:

```javascript
const service = require('./services/multiRecipientService');

const share = await service.createMultiRecipientShare({
  encryptedPassword: encrypt(password),
  expirationMinutes: 120,
  recipients: ['user1@example.com', 'user2@example.com'],
  maxAccessCount: 10,
  description: 'Database credentials'
});

await service.revokeRecipient(share.share._id, 'user1@example.com', userId, 'Left team');
```

## Resolves #696

This implementation fully addresses the enhancement request for multi-recipient sharing with individual revocation control.

title: Fix: Add comprehensive password content validation to prevent malware distribution
body:
## Summary

Implements multi-layer password content validation to detect and prevent malware distribution through password sharing. This addresses the security gap where users could inadvertently share malicious content (executables, scripts, URLs) disguised as passwords.

## Changes

### New Validators & Middleware
- **PasswordContentValidator** (`src/validation/passwordContent.validation.js`): Core validation engine with 20+ security rules
  - Detects executable files (.exe, .bat, .sh, .jar, .zip, etc.)
  - Identifies URLs masquerading as passwords
  - Blocks script protocols (javascript:, vbscript:, data:)
  - Rejects file system paths
  - Prevents blocked dangerous commands (cmd.exe, powershell.exe, rm -rf, etc.)
  - Calculates security levels (high/medium/low)
  - Detects homograph attacks using similar-looking characters

- **validatePasswordContent middleware** (`src/middleware/validatePasswordContent.js`): Express middleware for easy integration into password sharing endpoints
  - Validates password content before sharing
  - Returns detailed error and warning messages
  - Attaches security metadata to response context

### Comprehensive Test Coverage
- **28 test cases** covering all validation scenarios
  - Valid password acceptance
  - Executable file rejection
  - URL detection and blocking
  - Script/protocol blocking
  - File path rejection
  - Blocked content patterns
  - Size limit enforcement (10KB)
  - Warning patterns (URL-like, IP addresses)
  - Security level calculation
  - Edge cases (empty, null, unicode)

## How to Integrate

Add middleware to password sharing routes:

```javascript
const validatePasswordContent = require('./middleware/validatePasswordContent');

app.post('/api/share-password', 
  validatePasswordContent,
  sharePasswordController
);
```

## Validation Features

- **Malware Prevention**: Blocks attempts to share executables and scripts
- **User Warnings**: Alerts for suspicious content (IP addresses, URL-like patterns)
- **Security Scoring**: Evaluates password strength
- **Homograph Detection**: Warns about lookalike character combinations (0/O, 1/l/I, 8/B)
- **Size Validation**: Enforces 10KB maximum password length

## Testing

All tests pass with comprehensive coverage:
- Valid credentials accepted
- Malicious patterns blocked
- Warnings generated for suspicious content
- Edge cases handled properly

Resolves #697

title: fix: prevent unlimited anonymous reviews from manipulating average rating
body:
> 🌟 This PR is part of **GirlScript Summer of Code 2026 (GSSoC '26)**

## Summary
Fixes #700

Fixes the issue where unlimited anonymous review submissions could inflate 
or deflate the displayed average rating. Previously, testimonials were stored 
only in `localStorage` with no server-side checks, so the same user could 
submit multiple reviews under different names and each one affected the average.

This PR moves reviews to a shared backend API and adds spam-prevention controls 
so ratings reflect genuine feedback more reliably.

Also fixes backend startup crash by replacing the stub user.model.js with a proper Mongoose model, required for the server to run and serve the testimonials API.

## Changes

### Backend
- Added `Testimonial` MongoDB model with approval status and client fingerprint tracking
- New API routes:
  - `GET /api/testimonials` — fetch approved reviews + average rating stats
  - `POST /api/testimonials` — submit a new review (rate-limited)
  - `PUT /api/testimonials` — update an existing review for the same device
- Spam prevention:
  - **One review per device** via unique `clientFingerprint`
  - **IP rate limiting** (5 attempts/hour)
  - **Daily IP cap** (max 3 new reviews per IP per 24h)
  - **Duplicate comment detection** (same IP + same comment within 24h)
  - **Honeypot field** to block basic bots
  - Server-side validation for name, rating, and comment length
- Seeded default testimonials on first load when the collection is empty

### Frontend
- Replaced `localStorage`-only review storage with `testimonialService` API integration
- Persistent browser fingerprint (`reviewClient.js`) to enforce one review per device
- Users who already submitted see **Edit Your Review** instead of creating duplicates
- Added success/error messaging and bilingual form labels (EN/HI)
- Graceful fallback to sample testimonials if the API is unavailable (read-only)

## Why This Approach
| Approach | Benefit |
|----------|---------|
| Server-side storage | Shared, consistent ratings for all users |
| Fingerprint uniqueness | Stops casual repeat submissions from the same browser |
| Edit-in-place (PUT) | Users can update their review without adding a new one |
| IP limits + honeypot | Reduces automated spam and rating manipulation |
| Backend validation | Client-side checks alone can be bypassed |

## Test Plan
- [ ] Start backend with MongoDB running
- [ ] Set `VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:5005/api` in `frontend/.env`
- [ ] Open homepage testimonials section — seed reviews load with average rating
- [ ] Submit a review → success message, rating updates
- [ ] Try submitting again with a different name → blocked with message to edit existing review
- [ ] Click **Edit Your Review** → update rating/comment → changes save successfully
- [ ] Verify average rating only changes when a review is added/updated, not duplicated
- [ ] Confirm honeypot: filling hidden `website` field rejects submission (API test)
- [ ] Confirm UI still shows sample reviews when backend is offline (fallback mode)

## Notes
- Review submission requires the backend API; fallback mode is read-only
- Future improvement: optional admin moderation queue (`pending` → `approved`) 
  if moderation UI is added later

## Type of Change
- [x] Bug fix
- [x] New feature (spam prevention, fingerprinting)
- [ ] Breaking change

Bumps [axios](https://github.com/axios/axios) from 1.16.1 to 1.17.0.
Release notes

Sourced from axios's releases.

v1.17.0 — June 1, 2026

This release adds Node HTTP zstd decompression, hardens config and release workflows, and fixes authentication, header, proxy, and type-handling regressions.

🔒 Security Fixes

  • Config Hardening: Guarded socketPath, params, and paramsSerializer reads with own-property checks to prevent inherited prototype values from affecting request behavior, including SSRF-sensitive paths. (#10901, #10922)
  • Release Publishing: Switched the publish workflow to npm staged publishing for safer, auditable package releases with provenance. (#10926)

🚀 New Features

  • HTTP Compression: Added Node HTTP adapter support for zstd response decompression, with transitional.advertiseZstdAcceptEncoding controlling whether zstd is advertised in Accept-Encoding. (#6792, #10920)

🐛 Bug Fixes

  • Authentication Handling: Restored Basic auth on same-origin Node redirects while continuing to strip credentials cross-origin, and aligned the fetch adapter with HTTP adapter behavior for URL-embedded Basic auth. (#10929, #10896)
  • Proxy TLS: Preserved user httpsAgent TLS options when tunneling HTTPS requests through HTTP CONNECT proxies. (#10957)
  • React Native FormData: Cleared default Content-Type for React Native FormData so multipart boundaries can be generated correctly. (#10898)
  • Headers: Silently skipped empty or whitespace-only header names instead of throwing, matching parsed-header behavior and avoiding React Native response crashes. (#10875)
  • Request Data Merging: Preserved enumerable symbol keys when cloning plain request data through axios merge logic. (#10812)
  • Bundler Compatibility: Converted resolveConfig from an arrow default export to a named function export to avoid webpack and Babel transform interop failures. (#10891)
  • Types: Corrected AxiosHeaders.toJSON() return types and updated CommonJS isCancel typings to narrow to CanceledError<T>. (#10956, #10952)
  • Build Tooling: Avoided emitting a null Authorization header from the GitHub build helper when GITHUB_TOKEN is unset. (#10931)

🔧 Maintenance & Chores

  • HTTP/2 Internals: Extracted Http2Sessions into its own helper module and added direct unit coverage for session pooling, timeout, and cleanup behavior. (#10861)
  • Package Publishing: Reduced published package size by switching to a files allowlist and dropping unneeded unminified bundle source maps. (#10939)
  • CI and Release Automation: Added bundle-size reporting, moved reports to the job summary, fixed bundle-size comparison coverage, added Node 26 to the matrix, pinned npm for staged publishing, and prepared the 1.17.0 release. (#10907, #10911, #10916, #10927, #10935, #10983)

body:
Bumps [fuse.js](https://github.com/krisk/Fuse) from 7.3.0 to 7.4.2.
Release notes

Sourced from fuse.js's releases.

v7.4.2

Bug Fixes

  • types: ship CommonJS type declarations (.d.cts) so consumers on moduleResolution: node16/nodenext no longer hit TS1479 ("masquerading as ESM") when importing the package from a CommonJS project. The lib entries now resolve to a runtime-accurate export = declaration and the worker to its named declaration, via the require exports condition. (#780)

Full Changelog: https://github.com/krisk/Fuse/compare/v7.4.1...v7.4.2

v7.4.1

Bug Fixes

  • types: ship TypeScript declarations for fuse.js/worker (572ad1e), closes #828
  • types: add TypeScript declarations for fuse.js/worker-script (6ef6c33), closes #828

Both worker subpaths now resolve types under node16/nodenext and bundler module resolution.

v7.4.0

First stable on the 7.4 line. Aggregates everything from the 7.4.0-beta.1 → 7.4.0-beta.8 cycle. See CHANGELOG.md for the per-beta breakdown.

Features

  • FuseWorker — parallel search via Web Workers (9ba192c). New FuseWorker class shards the collection across workers, runs searches in parallel, and preserves the same result ordering as Fuse. See the Web Workers guide.
  • token-search: tokenMatch: 'all' | 'any' option for AND/OR semantics (#827, 9f979d0). Default 'any' preserves existing behavior; 'all' requires every query word to match somewhere in a record (term-centric AND), useful for filter-style queries.
  • token-search: customizable tokenizer with unicode-aware default (#821, 8e55cae). New tokenize?: RegExp | (text: string) => string[] option; the default regex now segments CJK, Cyrillic, Greek, Arabic, etc. out of the box.

Bug Fixes

  • matches: report array-path keys as dotted strings (acd54e8). Previously match.key leaked a raw string[] for keys declared as path arrays (e.g. keys: [['author', 'firstName']]), contradicting the documented FuseResultMatch.key: string type. Now emits the canonical dotted string ('author.firstName'). Behavior change: consumers relying on the array shape need to update.
  • bitap: restrict highlight indices to matched window (#792, 622f105). Closes #505, #611, #691, #793.
  • index: correct doc-index alignment for Fuse<string> with blank docs (0b8e3ca).
  • core: invalidate searcher cache on collection mutation (fcf4228).
  • token-search: renumber inverted index after doc removal (ea9356d).

body:
Bumps [axios](https://github.com/axios/axios) from 1.16.1 to 1.17.0.
Release notes

Sourced from axios's releases.

v1.17.0 — June 1, 2026

This release adds Node HTTP zstd decompression, hardens config and release workflows, and fixes authentication, header, proxy, and type-handling regressions.

🔒 Security Fixes

  • Config Hardening: Guarded socketPath, params, and paramsSerializer reads with own-property checks to prevent inherited prototype values from affecting request behavior, including SSRF-sensitive paths. (#10901, #10922)
  • Release Publishing: Switched the publish workflow to npm staged publishing for safer, auditable package releases with provenance. (#10926)

🚀 New Features

  • HTTP Compression: Added Node HTTP adapter support for zstd response decompression, with transitional.advertiseZstdAcceptEncoding controlling whether zstd is advertised in Accept-Encoding. (#6792, #10920)

🐛 Bug Fixes

  • Authentication Handling: Restored Basic auth on same-origin Node redirects while continuing to strip credentials cross-origin, and aligned the fetch adapter with HTTP adapter behavior for URL-embedded Basic auth. (#10929, #10896)
  • Proxy TLS: Preserved user httpsAgent TLS options when tunneling HTTPS requests through HTTP CONNECT proxies. (#10957)
  • React Native FormData: Cleared default Content-Type for React Native FormData so multipart boundaries can be generated correctly. (#10898)
  • Headers: Silently skipped empty or whitespace-only header names instead of throwing, matching parsed-header behavior and avoiding React Native response crashes. (#10875)
  • Request Data Merging: Preserved enumerable symbol keys when cloning plain request data through axios merge logic. (#10812)
  • Bundler Compatibility: Converted resolveConfig from an arrow default export to a named function export to avoid webpack and Babel transform interop failures. (#10891)
  • Types: Corrected AxiosHeaders.toJSON() return types and updated CommonJS isCancel typings to narrow to CanceledError<T>. (#10956, #10952)
  • Build Tooling: Avoided emitting a null Authorization header from the GitHub build helper when GITHUB_TOKEN is unset. (#10931)

🔧 Maintenance & Chores

  • HTTP/2 Internals: Extracted Http2Sessions into its own helper module and added direct unit coverage for session pooling, timeout, and cleanup behavior. (#10861)
  • Package Publishing: Reduced published package size by switching to a files allowlist and dropping unneeded unminified bundle source maps. (#10939)
  • CI and Release Automation: Added bundle-size reporting, moved reports to the job summary, fixed bundle-size comparison coverage, added Node 26 to the matrix, pinned npm for staged publishing, and prepared the 1.17.0 release. (#10907, #10911, #10916, #10927, #10935, #10983)
  • Developer Workflow: Added a dev container and iterated on OpenSpec workflow files before removing them from the release branch. (#10925, #10914, #10958)

  • Dependencies: Bumped Babel tooling, Commitlint, ESLint, Rollup, Globals, Vitest, Playwright, fs-extra, qs, docs dependencies, and GitHub Actions dependencies including actions/dependency-review-action and zizmorcore/zizmor-action. body:
Bumps [redis](https://github.com/redis/node-redis) from 4.7.1 to 6.0.0.
Release notes

Sourced from redis's releases.

redis@6.0.0

Changes

This is the first major release of node-redis since 5.x. The headline change is RESP3 by default, alongside broader Redis 8.8 command coverage, pubsub/cluster reliability fixes, and a bumped minimum Node.js version.

Key changes

  • RESP3 is the default protocol — opt back into RESP2 explicitly if you need the old wire format
  • Node.js 20 is the new minimum supported runtime
  • Redis 8.8 coverage — new array commands, INCREX / INCREXBYFLOAT, ZINTER/ZUNION COUNT aggregator, XNACK, CLIENT UNBLOCK
  • Sentinel & cluster pubsub fixes for failover-moved connections and sharded topology recovery
  • Stack modules exposed on pool type and the core client classes are now publicly re-exported

🔥 Breaking Changes

See the v5 → v6 migration guide for migration steps.

  • RESP3 is now the default protocol (#3215)
  • Node.js 20 is now the minimum supported version (#3293)
  • New default values for keepAliveInitialDelay (30s) and commandTimeout (5s) (#3292)

🔒 Security

  • entraid: bump @azure/msal-node to 5.x to drop vulnerable transitive uuid (CVE-2026-41907) (#3269)

🚀 New Features

  • Add CLIENT UNBLOCK command (#3266)
  • Add XNACK command with options (#3238)
  • Add Redis 8.8 array commands (#3285)
  • Add INCREX and INCREXBYFLOAT commands (#3288)
  • Add COUNT aggregator to ZINTER / ZINTERSTORE / ZUNION / ZUNIONSTORE (#3243)
  • Add FPHA option to JSON.SET (#3235)
  • time-series: add multi-aggregation range APIs (#3249)
  • Add duplicate() method to RedisSentinel (#3212)
  • Parse unix:// URLs in parseURL (#3271)
  • Expose RedisClient, RedisCluster, RedisSentinel and pool classes (#3251)
  • Expose stack modules on the pool type (#3267)

🐛 Bug Fixes

  • sentinel: use mapped address when failover moves pubsub connections (#3190)
  • cluster: recover sharded pubsub topology after node reconnects (#3223)
  • client: swallow synchronous EPIPE from writeAfterFIN (#3283)
  • search: allow LOAD * in FT.AGGREGATE (#3241)
  • otel: record client connection errors (#3259)
  • entraid: bump @azure/msal-node to 5.x to drop vulnerable uuid transitive (#3269)

title: [FEATURE] Enhance Pull Request Template with Robust Quality Checklist
body:
## What
Overhaul the PR template with a comprehensive quality checklist covering testing environments, visual proof, security compliance, responsiveness, and accessibility.

## Root cause
The existing PR template is basic and lacks fields for environment verification, responsiveness checks, accessibility standards, and a visual proof table.

## Changes
- Added **Testing Environment** section with backend/frontend/AI service/mobile checkboxes
- Added **Visual Proof** table for side-by-side before/after screenshots
- Added **Security Compliance** section (secrets, validation, auth, env vars)
- Added **Responsiveness & Accessibility** section (ARIA, focus indicators, color contrast)
- Expanded final **Checklist** with a test requirement

## Verification
- Open a new PR on the repo
- The enhanced template appears with all new sections

## CI failures
N/A - docs/config change only.

## Before
Basic template with 3 sections: Description, Type of Change, Checklist.

## After
Enhanced template with 6 sections: Description, Type of Change, Testing Environment, Visual Proof, Security Compliance, Responsiveness & Accessibility, Checklist.

Closes #462
